title: Din Nuuk până-n Focșani, românii-s americani!
summary: The article critiques AUR leader George Simion's embrace of Trumpism, from cutting a Greenland-shaped cake at a Republican celebration to his party's disruption of Romania's 1859 Union Day ceremonies. The author argues that Romanian 'patriots' have imported American political tactics, particularly Trump's strategy of delegitimizing elected leaders.
talking_points: George Simion attended a Republican anniversary event celebrating Trump's return to the White House, where he cut a Greenland-shaped cake decorated with the American flag
AUR militants disrupted official Union Day celebrations in Focșani and Iași by booing official speeches and claiming the president is illegitimate
The author draws a parallel between AUR's refusal to recognize Romania's current president and Trump's refusal to recognize Biden's 2020 victory
Trump's Greenland threats caused NATO tensions but he backed down after negative stock market reactions at Davos
Romanian self-proclaimed sovereignists are paradoxically acting as American political proxies
author: Cristian Preda
